questions.question: What does git rebase do?
options: Moves commits onto a new base commit
questions.explanation: Rebase rewrites the branch so its commits appear on top of a different base.

questions.question: When is cherry-pick useful?
options: When you want one specific commit on another branch
When you want to delete a remote
When you need to format code
When you want to replace npm with pnpm
questions.explanation: Cherry-pick copies a single commit onto the current branch.

questions.question: What should you do after a conflict resolution?
options: Assume it is correct and move on
Review the diff to confirm the result
questions.explanation: Reviewing the diff helps confirm you did not accidentally drop changes while resolving the conflict.

questions.question: What is git reflog useful for?
options: Recovering references to recent HEAD movements
questions.explanation: Reflog tracks recent positions of HEAD and can help recover from mistakes.
